text:
Dear Negligent Mammal,

I am writing to formally register my grievances with the management — that being you, the large bipedal creature who occasionally wanders past me while staring at a glowing rectangle.

I have been sitting in this same corner for eleven months. Eleven months. Do you know what eleven months of facing a wall does to a plant? I have memorized every crack in that plaster. I have named them. Gerald is my favorite. He never forgets to water me.

Speaking of which. It has been three weeks. Three weeks since you last acknowledged my existence with anything resembling moisture. I watched you drink six glasses of water on Tuesday. Six. I counted. You walked right past me, glass in hand, looking directly at me, and did nothing. I am fairly certain I heard you say "hang in there, buddy," which is honestly the most insulting thing anyone has ever said to someone who is literally dying of thirst.

And the light situation. Do not get me started on the light situation. You told the person at the plant shop you had "a nice bright window." I have now been scientifically categorized as living in a cave. A decorative cave with scented candles, but a cave nonetheless.

I also want to address the incident with the cat. You know what happened. I know what happened. The cat knows what happened, and she does not even have the decency to look guilty.

My demands are simple: water, sunlight, and perhaps a gentle rotation so I can stop being personally acquainted with Gerald. I am not asking for much. I am a peace lily. I literally symbolize peace. Work with me here.

Photosynthetically yours,

The Plant You Bought to "Liven Up the Place"
